name: Pioz and Pezuela de las Torres Loop
name_original: Pioz - Pezuela de las Torres – circular por El Tesoro
description: This easy 14.0-mile (22.5 km) mountain biking route near Pioz offers scenic views and accessible terrain, perfect for all skill levels.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Pioz traverse a landscape characterized by agricultural fields, scattered woodlands, and gentle rolling terrain. The region features a mix of open paths and tracks that connect small towns and historical sites. Elevation changes are generally moderate, making it accessible for various skill levels. The area provides opportunities to explore rural Spain on two wheels. Best mountain bike trails around Pioz The most popular mountain bike trail is Pioz and Pezuela de las Torres Loop, an easy 14.0 miles (22.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 38 minutes to complete. This route offers a straightforward ride through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Valmores Fountain – Rocky Descent loop from Urbanización Las Matillas, a moderate 23.3 miles (37.5 km) path. This trail features varied terrain, including some rocky sections and open views. Local mountain bikers also love the Church of Santorcaz – Pioz Castle loop from Pezuela de las Torres, a 26.2 miles (42.2 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past historical points of interest, often completed in about 3 hours 32 minutes. Mountain biking around Pioz is defined by open fields, pine forests, and connecting routes between villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family-friendly rides to more challenging moderate paths. The routes in Pioz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 180 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Pioz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Pioz?
A: There are over 25 mountain bike trails around Pioz, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Pioz?
A: The mountain bike trails around Pioz are predominantly easy to moderate. You'll find 7 easy trails, perfect for beginners or a relaxed ride, and 19 moderate trails for those seeking a bit more challenge. There are no difficult trails listed in the immediate area.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike routes in Pioz?
A: Yes, Pioz offers several family-friendly mountain bike routes. Many of the easy trails are suitable for families, characterized by gentle rolling terrain and open paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006143374/pioz-and-pezuela-de-las-torres-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pioz and Pezuela de las Torres Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 14.0-mile (22.5 km) route that provides a straightforward ride through the local countryside.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on mountain bike trails near Pioz?
A: Mountain bike trails around Pioz traverse a landscape of agricultural fields, scattered woodlands, and gentle rolling terrain. You'll often ride through open fields and pine forests, connecting small towns and historical sites. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19115803" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabla Pintora Pine Forest – Puerta de la Cadena loop from Pioz&lt;/a&gt; offer a good mix of these natural elements.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike trails available in Pioz?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Pioz are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006143374/pioz-and-pezuela-de-las-torres-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pioz and Pezuela de las Torres Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19092898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santorcaz – Pioz Castle loop from Pezuela de las Torres&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or interesting landmarks along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Pioz offer glimpses of historical and natural landmarks. You might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2409049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pioz Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3501590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmores Fountain&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/22075998" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmores Fountain – Rocky Descent loop from Urbanización Las Matillas&lt;/a&gt; specifically features open views and varied terrain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Pioz?
A: The mountain bike routes in Pioz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 18 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, from open fields to pine forests, and the well-connected routes between villages, making it enjoyable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through lavender fields near Pioz?
A: Yes, you can find trails that lead through picturesque lavender fields. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38251396" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Locked gate at PK 65.150 – Lavender Field loop from Pozo de Guadalajara&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 38.0 km route that offers this unique scenic experience, particularly beautiful during bloom season.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Pioz?
A: Given the agricultural landscape and open fields, spring and autumn are generally ideal for mountain biking in Pioz. The temperatures are milder, and the scenery is vibrant, with blooming fields in spring or rich colors in autumn. Summer can be very hot, and some trails might be muddy after heavy winter rains.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions accessible by mountain bike near Pioz?
A: Yes, several routes pass by or near historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19092898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santorcaz – Pioz Castle loop from Pezuela de las Torres&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads through rural landscapes and past historical points of interest, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2409049" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pioz Castle&lt;/a&gt;. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goyeneche Palace of Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt; in the wider area.
tips: The island surrounded by green wheat is the tip of an area called El Tesoro ... now let your imagination fly
If you have gone up to the urbanization to look out over the plain from the forest watchtower, when you return, do not run because the views of Pezuela de las Torres are beautiful.

Interesting work of the s. XV, which allows us to appreciate how a castle was arranged when the use of artillery had already been imposed: harquebuses and cannons. It is erected on a plain, and preserves both its interior and towers, as well as the outer enclosure and the moat.

Free access to the outside. To visit the interior, contact the Pioz town hall, telephone 949.272.076.

faqs.question: Is the Pioz - Pezuela de las Torres – circular por El Tesoro trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is classified as easy and is highly suitable for beginners, families, and cyclists of all fitness levels. Its moderate length and relatively low elevation gain make it an excellent choice for a relaxed ride without requiring advanced technical skills.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of straightforward tracks, paths, and trails. You'll encounter a mix of granite and sandy ground, which is advantageous as it drains quickly, reducing the likelihood of muddy conditions even after rain. The route also passes through pine forests and quarries, offering varied scenery without significant inclines.

faqs.question: What interesting sights or landmarks can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can enjoy several notable points of interest. These include the El Tesoro Track, a scenic View of Pezuela de las Torres, and the historic Pioz Castle.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Pioz - Pezuela de las Torres – circular por El Tesoro trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, you can typically find parking in the towns of Pioz or Pezuela de las Torres, which serve as access points for the trail. Look for public parking options within the town centers.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally dog-friendly, allowing you to bring your canine companion along for the ride. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when encountering other cyclists, hikers, or wildlife, and to adhere to local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Pioz - Pezuela de las Torres – circular por El Tesoro trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the granite and sandy terrain that drains quickly, this route can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking in this region, avoiding the intense heat of summer or potential chill of winter.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Pioz and Pezuela de las Torres are towns where you can find local cafes, restaurants, and pubs to refuel before or after your ride. These towns offer amenities for visitors.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You will ride along sections of the Camino Cañada Real, Ruta Circular: Sector Este R-C Circular, Cañada de Merinas, and Camino de Corpa, among others.

faqs.question: How can I get the GPX track for this route?
faqs.answer: You can easily download the GPX track for the Pioz - Pezuela de las Torres – circular por El Tesoro route directly from its Komoot tour page. This will allow you to navigate the trail using your preferred GPS device or app.

faqs.question: What kind of natural scenery can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse landscape, alternating between the provinces of Madrid and Guadalajara. You'll pass through pine forests, open scrubland rich in rockrose, lavender, and thyme, and areas with granite formations, providing a pleasant natural backdrop for your ride.

waypoints.name: View of Pezuela de las Torres
